title: Running a GDP Self-Inspection
author: B. Subramanian
excerpt: A senior QP's practical guide to running a GDP self inspection: scope, independent auditors, ALCOA+ records, CAPA and staying MHRA inspection-ready.
cover_image_prompt: A photorealistic clinical-luxury banner of a pristine pharmaceutical wholesale distribution warehouse, soft natural daylight raking across orderly racks of temperature-controlled stock as a quality auditor's gloved hand holds a calibrated data logger and a clipboard in crisp focus, the aisle of shelving dissolving into a creamy shallow-depth-of-field blur behind. Cool teal and cyan tones wash the immaculate space against deep navy shadows, lifted by a subtle green accent glow from a wall-mounted temperature monitor. Calm, premium, meticulous and orderly, 4:3 aspect ratio, no text, no logos.

faqs.q: How often should a GDP self-inspection be carried out?
faqs.a: EU and UK GDP expect self-inspections to be conducted on a planned basis covering the whole quality system, and for most wholesalers an annual cycle is the practical baseline. Frequency should be risk-based, so cold-chain operations, newly licensed activities or areas with recent excursions or recalls warrant more frequent attention. The schedule should be defined in a procedure and supplemented by for-cause or unannounced inspections where intelligence suggests a problem.

faqs.q: Who can perform a GDP self-inspection?
faqs.a: Self-inspections should be carried out by competent, trained persons who are objective about the area under review, and the cardinal rule is that an auditor must not assess work for which they are personally responsible. You can use staff from another function, peer auditors from a sister site, or an external GDP specialist. Whoever performs it must understand both GDP and your specific processes well enough to test them rather than simply confirm them.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a GDP self-inspection and an MHRA inspection?
faqs.a: A GDP self-inspection is internal: your own organisation examines its distribution operation against GDP and feeds findings into CAPA and management review. An MHRA inspection is external and determines whether your site retains its Wholesale Dealer's Authorisation. A strong self-inspection programme is precisely what makes an MHRA inspection a confirmation of control rather than a source of unwelcome surprises.
